mission.title: backstage: fix: fix template parsing issue preventing proper function of RJSF allOf / oneOf / anyOf with nested if / else fields
mission.description:
A fellow coworker once opened an issue about RJSF's `allOf` / `oneOf` / `anyOf` properties not working when they're defined in the root of a Scaffolder template step. I've done some digging since we need those features to work soon, I've actually managed to pinpoint the cause of the problem, which is located in the Scaffolder's template parsing when loading the schema in the Scaffolder's `Stepper` component.

The cause of the issue is in the Scaffolder's frontend plugin, `plugin-scaffolder-react` where it loads a template from the catalog and parses it. The parsing is done in the `useTemplateSchema` hook where it strips a title for every one of the steps, since the step is also shown in the `Stepper` component at the top of the page. 

However, when stripping the title, it is explicitly set to `undefined` which seemingly causes an issue with one of RJSF's internal validators. This invalidates (certain parts of) the schema for the template and causes `allOf` / `oneOf` / `anyOf` properti

resolution.summary:

steps: Review the issue discussion for context
Apply the fix from the linked pull request
